text: file provides guidance Codex (Codex.ai/code) working code repository.

title: AGENTS.md
text: EpiModel R package simulating mathematical models infectious disease dynamics. distinguishing capability stochastic network epidemic modeling grounded Exponential-family Random Graph Models (ERGMs). supports three model classes: Deterministic Compartmental Models (DCMs) – ODE-based (using deSolve). Population-level, continuous-time, stochasticity. Stochastic Individual Contact Models (ICMs) – discrete-time agent-based random mixing. persistent network structure. Stochastic Network Models – primary focus. Uses ERGMs/TERGMs represent dynamic contact networks partnerships form, persist, dissolve according statistically estimated rules. model class follows unified API: param.*(), init.*(), control.*() inputs, dcm(), icm(), netsim() run simulations. .data.frame, plot, summary, print S3 methods. Built-compartmental structures: SI, SIR, SIS, one-group two-group configurations, without demography. extension API allows arbitrary disease states.

title: AGENTS.md
text: modifying Roxygen2 comments (#' blocks) @export tags, re-run roxygen2::roxygenise() regenerate NAMESPACE man/ files testing. modifying C/C++ code src/, package must reinstalled (R CMD INSTALL .) testing.
code: # Install package locally (with compiled C/C++ code) R CMD INSTALL .  # Generate documentation from Roxygen2 comments (updates NAMESPACE and man/) Rscript -e 'roxygen2::roxygenise()'  # Full package check -- the primary way to test (tests + examples + CRAN compliance) # This runs all unit tests in tests/ AND all examples in man pages R CMD build . && _R_CHECK_FORCE_SUGGESTS_=false R CMD check --as-cran --no-manual EpiModel_*.tar.gz  # Run a single test file (must load the package first for namespace access) Rscript -e 'library(EpiModel); testthat::test_file("tests/testthat/test-dcm.R")'  # Lint the package Rscript -e 'lintr::lint_package()'

title: AGENTS.md
text: Use R CMD check canonical test method. runs unit tests via testthat::test_check() (properly loads package namespace) runs documentation examples. need separately run test_check test_package – R CMD check already covers . use testthat::test_dir("tests/testthat") – load package namespace, internal/unexported functions (e.g., tedgelist_to_toggles, name_saveout_elts) re-exported imports (e.g., networkLite) found, causing spurious test failures. Use _R_CHECK_FORCE_SUGGESTS_=false skip optional Suggests packages may installed (e.g., ergm.ego, ndtv, egor). Long-running tests (test-net-long.R, test-icm-long.R, test-dcm-long.R) guarded skip_on_cran(). R CMD check ---cran skips ; drop ---cran locally touching core simulation code want coverage.

title: AGENTS.md

title: AGENTS.md
text: R/net.inputs.R (largest file): Validates processes network model inputs (param.net, init.net, control.net) R/net.fn.utils.R, R/net.fn.accessor.R: Core utilities attribute accessors internal dat simulation object R/get.R: Public API extracting simulation results (get_param, get_attr, get_epi, etc.) R/edgelists.R: Edgelist manipulation cumulative edgelist tracking R/dcm.mods.R: Built-ODE systems standard SI/SIR/SIS compartmental models

title: AGENTS.md
text: Network simulations pass central dat list object modules. Since v2.5 restart refactor, runtime state lives dat$run — dat$run alone sufficient checkpoint resume simulation. Runtime state (dat$run): - dat$run$attr - Node-level attributes (disease status, demographics, etc.) - dat$run$el - Edgelists per network layer (tergmLite mode) - dat$run$nw - Network objects per network layer (non-tergmLite mode) - dat$run$net_attr - Per-network metadata (size, lasttoggle) - dat$run$nwterms - Nodal attributes referenced ERGM formula - dat$run$last_unique_id - Counter newly appended nodes Top-level (static aggregated): - dat$epi - Epidemic tracking variables (counts per time step) - dat$stats - Network-statistic tracking (dat$stats$nwstats, dat$stats$transmat) - dat$nwparam - Network model parameters - dat$num.nw - Number network layers - dat$param / dat$init / dat$control - User-provided simulation configuration Prefer accessor API (get_attr/set_attr, get_network/set_network, get_param, get_epi/set_epi, get_control, append_core_attr/append_attr) direct dat$run$* manipulation. #977 migrated internal code direction, accessors handle validation, lasttoggle / unique_id bookkeeping, tergmLite vs. non-tergmLite dispatch.

title: AGENTS.md
text: plot.icm (R/plot.icm.R) plot_netsim_epi (R/plot.netsim.R) sibling plotting paths stochastic-epidemic output — different data-generating mechanisms, summary statistics. share draw_means() / draw_qnts() R/plot.R stay structurally symmetric possible. Treat behavioral divergences bugs lagging side, design. Remaining known asymmetries tracked #1012. plot.dcm (R/plot.dcm.R) intentionally independent — different time model, uses control$timesteps x-coordinates directly. .data.frame.netsim delegates entirely .data.frame.icm, changes ICM method automatically apply netsim.

what: Time Handling Differences Across Model Classes
title: AGENTS.md
text: DCM: control$timesteps stores explicit time vector (may non-integer dt < 1). control$nsteps max time value. control$nruns number sensitivity runs, derived longest vector-valued argument param.dcm() init.dcm() (initial-condition sensitivity added #408). Default ODE solver "lsoda" (changed "rk4" v2.6.0 numerical stability). ICM/netsim: Time implicit via row indices. control$nsteps number rows max time step. control$nsims number stochastic simulations. control$start provides time offset (default 1). modifying functions touch time epi structure, verify behavior across three classes.

title: AGENTS.md
text: Models can use multiple network layers (e.g., sexual + needle-sharing networks). Network parameters, edgelists, diagnostics indexed network number. See test-multinets.R examples.

title: AGENTS.md
text: package heavily depends Statnet ecosystem: ergm, tergm, network, networkDynamic, networkLite, statnet.common. Understanding ERGM model specification important working network model code. Parallelization uses future / future.apply framework (switched foreach/doParallel v2.6.0). netsim() netdx() default multisession; users can override plan via future.use.plan control argument.

title: AGENTS.md
text: sections provide broader context EpiModel’s epidemiological modeling concepts. helps working features, writing documentation, understanding purpose behind code patterns.

title: AGENTS.md
text: Traditional epidemic models assume random (“mass-action”) mixing. Real contact patterns structured – people fixed number partners, partnerships durations, clustering, assortative mixing, concurrency (overlapping partnerships). Network models capture features: degree distributions, relational durations, concurrency, assortative mixing attributes, clustering, bidirectional feedback network structure epidemic dynamics.

title: AGENTS.md
text: core workflow network epidemic model:
code: # 1. Initialize network nw <- network_initialize(n = 500) nw <- set_vertex_attribute(nw, "risk", rep(0:1, each = 250))  # 2. Parameterize formation/dissolution (target edges = mean_degree * N / 2) formation <- ~edges + concurrent + degrange(from = 4) target.stats <- c(175, 110, 0) coef.diss <- dissolution_coefs(dissolution = ~offset(edges), duration = 50)  # 3. Estimate network model est <- netest(nw, formation, target.stats, coef.diss)  # 4. Diagnose fit dx <- netdx(est, nsims = 10, nsteps = 1000, ncores = 4)  # 5. Simulate epidemic param <- param.net(inf.prob = 0.4, act.rate = 2, rec.rate = 0.1) init <- init.net(i.num = 10) control <- control.net(type = "SIS", nsims = 5, nsteps = 500) sim <- netsim(est, param, init, control)

title: AGENTS.md
text: Dyad-independent (faster estimation): edges, nodefactor, nodematch (homophily, use diff=TRUE per-level), nodemix, nodecov, absdiff. Dyad-dependent (require MCMC): degree(k), concurrent (alias mindegree(2)), degrange(= k), triangles, gwesp. Dissolution models use offset() terms : ~offset(edges) homogeneous duration, add offset(nodematch("attr")) differential duration.

title: AGENTS.md
text: Transmission probability: finalProb = 1 - (1 - transProb)^actRate Dissolution adjustment mortality: dissolution_coefs(~offset(edges), duration = 40, d.rate = 0.001) Estimation methods: Approximation (edapprox = TRUE, default, best durations > 50) vs. direct STERGM (edapprox = FALSE, short durations ~10-25).

title: AGENTS.md
text: extension API allows custom models arbitrary disease states, demographics, interventions, feedback. used feature applied research. Extension support network-class — control.icm() longer accepts custom module functions, skip.check, additional ... arguments (removed #980 v2.6.1), ICMs restricted built-SI/SIR/SIS types. Users needing custom ICM-style dynamics migrate netsim(). Module function signature: function(dat, ) { ...; return(dat) } dat master state object current time step. Accessor functions: get_attr/set_attr (nodal attributes), get_param (parameters), get_epi/set_epi (epidemic stats), append_core_attr/append_attr (new nodes), discord_edgelist (S-pairs), set_transmat (transmission events). Required nodal attributes: active, status, infTime, entrTime, exitTime. Departure rule: Set active[ids] <- 0 exitTime[ids] <- . Arrival rule: Call append_core_attr(dat, , n.new) first, append_attr custom attribute. statusTime guard pattern: Prevent multiple transitions per time step idsElig <- (active == 1 & status == "e" & statusTime < ). Extension model setup: Set type = NULL control.net() pass custom module functions (e.g., infection.FUN = my_infect). Key control.net parameters: resimulate.network = TRUE (feedback/demography), tergmLite = TRUE (20-50x faster), epi.= "attr" (stratified stats), nwstats.formula (track network stats).

title: AGENTS.md
text: v2.6.1 (#992) completed removal long-deprecated argument names — now hard-error pointer current name. Use current names writing examples, docs, tests: b.rate / b.rate.g2 → .rate / .rate.g2 (param.dcm / param.icm / param.net) trans.rate / trans.rate.g2 → inf.prob / inf.prob.g2 .m2 parameter suffix → .g2 (e.g., .num.m2 → .num.g2) births.FUN / deaths.FUN → arrivals.FUN / departures.FUN (control.net) depend → resimulate.network (control.net)

title: AGENTS.md
text: Network models can incorporate bidirectional feedback: demography (births/deaths reshape network), serosorting (using status ERGM term), behavioral interventions (reduced act rates diagnosed individuals), built-interventions (inter.eff, inter.start). status used ERGM formation formula, must initialized directly network object (via init.net). Network statistics preserved target levels – log-odds conditional terms preserved, statistics shift prevalence changes.

title: AGENTS.md
text: .data.frame.icm / .data.frame.netsim return objects carry epi.data.frame class alongside data.frame; plot.epi.data.frame plots like plot.netsim(type = "epi"). Build manually .epi.data.frame(df) df2epi(x). exported accessors helpers worth knowing: Simulation slicing: get_sims() (subset index), get_param_set() get_attr_history() (recorded parameter attribute trajectories). Transmission / reachability: get_discordant_edgelist() (S–pairs — generic form, supply discordance attribute explicitly), get_forward_reachable() / get_backward_reachable() cumulative edgelists (set control.net(save.cumulative.edgelist = TRUE) capture ). Restart & truncation: truncate_sim() S3 generic dcm / icm / netsim reset.time argument; make_restart_point() trims netsim minimum state needed resume. Attribute utilities: overwrite_attrs() applies init_attr data frame initialization; get_core_attributes() lists core attributes types (#969). Parameter tables: param.net_from_table() / param.net_to_table() convert list data.frame parameter representations.
code: as.data.frame(sim)                            # per-simulation raw data as.data.frame(sim, out = "mean")              # means across simulations get_network(sim, sim = 1)                     # extract network object get_transmat(sim, sim = 1)                    # transmission matrix mutate_epi(sim, ir = (si.flow / s.num) * 100) # derived statistics plot(sim)                                      # compartment sizes over time

title: EpiModel: Mathematical Modeling of Infectious Disease Dynamics
text: EpiModel package provides tools simulating mathematical models infectious disease dynamics. supports three model classes: Deterministic Compartmental Models (DCMs): ODE-based, population-level models rapid exploration epidemic dynamics simplifying assumptions. Stochastic Individual-Contact Models (ICMs): Agent-based models random mixing stochastic variability. Stochastic Network Models: primary focus EpiModel. use exponential-family random graph models (ERGMs) Statnet suite represent dynamic contact networks partnerships form, persist, dissolve according statistically estimated rules. Network models capture features strongly influence transmission dynamics—degree distributions, partnership durations, concurrency, assortative mixing, clustering. three model classes share unified API: configure inputs param.*(), init.*(), control.*(), run simulations dcm(), icm(), netsim(). vignette walks full network model pipeline, network estimation epidemic simulation analysis.

title: EpiModel: Mathematical Modeling of Infectious Disease Dynamics
text: Network models begin specifying estimating contact network. define network 500 people, estimate ERGM produces networks target mean degree 0.7 (175 edges), limited concurrency (110 nodes 2+ partners), one 4+ partners. Partnerships last average 50 time steps.
code: library(EpiModel) set.seed(12345)  nw <- network_initialize(n = 500)  formation <- ~edges + concurrent + degrange(from = 4) target.stats <- c(175, 110, 0) coef.diss <- dissolution_coefs(   dissolution = ~offset(edges),   duration = 50   # mean partnership duration in time steps )  est <- netest(nw, formation, target.stats, coef.diss, verbose = FALSE) ## Warning: 'glpk' selected as the solver, but package 'Rglpk' is not available; ## falling back to 'lpSolveAPI'. This should be fine unless the sample size and/or ## the number of parameters is very big.

title: EpiModel: Mathematical Modeling of Infectious Disease Dynamics

code: dx <- netdx(est, nsims = 5, nsteps = 500, verbose = FALSE) plot(dx) plot(dx, type = "duration")

title: EpiModel: Mathematical Modeling of Infectious Disease Dynamics

code: param <- param.net(inf.prob = 0.4, act.rate = 2, rec.rate = 0.05) init <- init.net(i.num = 10) control <- control.net(type = "SIS", nsims = 5, nsteps = 500, verbose = FALSE)  sim <- netsim(est, param, init, control)

title: EpiModel: Mathematical Modeling of Infectious Disease Dynamics
text: transmission matrix records every infection event: infected , , probability. can converted phylogenetic tree visualize chains transmission.  tip infected person; internal node (labeled infector’s ID) transmission event. horizontal axis shows time, revealing epidemic branched network.
code: # Run a separate SI model seeded with a single infection for a cleaner tree set.seed(42) param2 <- param.net(inf.prob = 0.5, act.rate = 2) init2 <- init.net(i.num = 5) control2 <- control.net(type = "SI", nsims = 1, nsteps = 60, verbose = FALSE) sim2 <- netsim(est, param2, init2, control2)  tm <- get_transmat(sim2) head(tm) ## # A tibble: 6 × 8 ## # Groups:   at, sus [6] ##      at   sus   inf network infDur transProb actRate finalProb ##   <int> <int> <int>   <int>  <dbl>     <dbl>   <dbl>     <dbl> ## 1     2   100    55       1     22       0.5       2      0.75 ## 2     2   500    55       1     22       0.5       2      0.75 ## 3     3   141   462       1     44       0.5       2      0.75 ## 4     4   175   141       1      1       0.5       2      0.75 ## 5     5   223   418       1      5       0.5       2      0.75 ## 6    16   414   418       1     16       0.5       2      0.75 tmPhylo <- as.phylo.transmat(tm) ## found multiple trees, returning a list of 3phylo objects par(mar = c(2, 1, 2, 1)) plot(tmPhylo, show.node.label = TRUE, root.edge = TRUE, cex = 0.5,      main = "Transmission Tree")

title: EpiModel: Mathematical Modeling of Infectious Disease Dynamics
text: built-SI, SIR, SIS models starting points. EpiModel’s extension API allows define custom models arbitrary disease states, demographics, interventions, feedback epidemic network. custom module simply function takes simulation state (dat) current time step (), modifies state, returns : Custom modules passed control.net integrated simulation loop. EpiModel Gallery provides library worked extension examples, advanced vignettes package document full API.
code: # Example: a custom progression module for an SEIR model progress_module <- function(dat, at) {   active <- get_attr(dat, "active")   status <- get_attr(dat, "status")    # E -> I transition   ids.EtoI <- which(active == 1 & status == "e" & runif(length(status)) < 0.1)   status[ids.EtoI] <- "i"    # I -> R transition   ids.ItoR <- which(active == 1 & status == "i" & runif(length(status)) < 0.02)   status[ids.ItoR] <- "r"    dat <- set_attr(dat, "status", status)   dat <- set_epi(dat, "ei.flow", at, length(ids.EtoI))   dat <- set_epi(dat, "ir.flow", at, length(ids.ItoR))   return(dat) }

title: Working with Custom Attributes and Summary Statistics in EpiModel
text: vignette covers work nodal attributes, attribute histories, epidemic summary statistics EpiModel network models custom extension modules. assumes familiarity setting running network models netsim() extension API. See Network Modeling Epidemics (NME) course materials EpiModel Gallery background. working network objects edgelists, see companion vignette Working Network Objects EpiModel. parameter management, see Working Model Parameters EpiModel. network simulations netsim, simulation state stored main list object (referred dat). vignette covers three types data dat: Current Nodal Attributes: Per-node characteristics current time step. Historical Nodal Attributes: Selective recordings attribute values time. Epidemic Trackers: Population-level summary statistics computed time step.

title: Working with Custom Attributes and Summary Statistics in EpiModel
text: Attributes characteristics nodes (e.g., persons) model current time step. stored vectors dat$attr, length number nodes.

title: Working with Custom Attributes and Summary Statistics in EpiModel
text: Every node four core attributes, managed automatically EpiModel: active: 1 node currently population, 0 departed. entrTime: time step node entered population. exitTime: time step node departed (NA still active). unique_id: globally unique integer identifier (never reused). can retrieve list core attributes default types get_core_attributes(): addition, built-epidemic modules core EpiModel use status attribute (disease status, values like "s", "", "r") infTime (time infection). complex disease-specific EpiModel implementations (e.g., HIV COVID) may handle disease status differently. Custom attributes name scalar value can added, age, race, viral_load.

title: Working with Custom Attributes and Summary Statistics in EpiModel
text: node can referenced two ways: position: Think like row number spreadsheet. dat$attr$active[3] accesses third node’s value directly. standard way look node information fast. unique_id: globally unique integer attribute assigned creation never reused. Slower look , allows referencing nodes already departed. Consider simple example. model starts 5 nodes: Node 3 departs step 10. vectors still 5 elements, active[3] = 0: new node arrives step 15. many simulations, departed nodes dropped attribute vectors, new node takes position 3 (freed slot) receives new unique_id = 6: Positional indices used current edgelists attribute accessors. Unique IDs used cumulative edgelists attribute histories can refer nodes longer model. See help("unique_id-tools", package = "EpiModel") conversion functions.

title: Working with Custom Attributes and Summary Statistics in EpiModel
text: minimal module increments age nodes 1: Key points set_attr(): returns modified copy dat, must assigned back. (cause performance issues due R’s shallow copy semantics since R 3.1.) replacement vector must length number nodes, function throws error.
code: aging_module <- function(dat, at) {    # Extract current attribute   age <- get_attr(dat, "age")    # Aging process   new_age <- age + 1    # Output updated attributes   dat <- set_attr(dat, "age", new_age)    return(dat) }

title: Working with Custom Attributes and Summary Statistics in EpiModel
text: Current attributes reflect state node present time step. Sometimes need track happened specific nodes time. saving full history attributes consume much memory, EpiModel provides selective recording mechanism: choose attributes, nodes, time steps record. useful diagnostics (verifying dynamic process coded correctly) tracking limited set individual-level outcomes analysis.

title: Working with Custom Attributes and Summary Statistics in EpiModel
text: following module records viral load infected nodes every 10 time steps. assumes model defines status attribute (values "s" susceptible, "" infected) viral_load attribute (continuous infected nodes, NA otherwise). record_attr_history() takes five arguments: dat: main list object. : time step associate recording (usually current step). label attribute ("viral_load"). vector posit_ids nodes interest. Internally, converted unique_ids history remains valid even nodes depart. values record IDs. number values must match number IDs, unless single value provided (recycled IDs, using less memory). Use attribute histories sparingly—storage can grow large open-population models many nodes many time steps.
code: viral_load_logger_module <- function(dat, at) {    # Run every 10 time steps   if (at %% 10 == 0) {      # Attributes     status <- get_attr(dat, "status")     viral_load <- get_attr(dat, "viral_load")      infected <- which(status == "i")      dat <- record_attr_history(       dat, at,       "viral_load",       infected,       viral_load[infected]     )   }    return(dat) }

title: Working with Custom Attributes and Summary Statistics in EpiModel
text: Attribute histories accessed simulation complete: result named list data.frames, one recorded attribute label. Suppose recorded "viral_load" () also "status" transitions another module. output look like: data.frame columns sim (simulation number), step (time step), attribute (label), uids (unique IDs), values (recorded values). list structure allows history different dimensions.
code: sim <- netsim(est, param, init, control) attr_history <- get_attr_history(sim) get_attr_history(sim)  # $viral_load #    sim step attribute    uids values # 1    1   10 viral_load   1001   2000 # 2    1   10 viral_load   1002   1878 # 3    1   20 viral_load   1001   1500 # 4    1   20 viral_load   1002    300 # ... # # $status #    sim step attribute  uids  values # 1    1   22 status     1001       i # 2    1   64 status     1002       i # 3    1  110 status     1001       r # 4    1  220 status     1002       r # ...

title: Working with Custom Attributes and Summary Statistics in EpiModel
text: Epidemic trackers population-level summary statistics computed stored time step—historical summary statistics population, individual nodes. example, built-tracker num records total population size step, s.num, .num, r.num record compartment sizes.

what: Working With Epidemic Trackers in Modules
title: Working with Custom Attributes and Summary Statistics in EpiModel
text: Inside module, access modify epidemic trackers get_epi() set_epi(). extended aging module also tracks mean age change time: Key points: set_epi() returns modified dat must assigned back. value must scalar. get_epi() retrieves tracker value specific time step. Requesting nonexistent tracker throws error.
code: aging_track_module <- function(dat, at) {    # Attributes   age <- get_attr(dat, "age")    # Aging process   new_age <- age + 1    # Calculate summary statistics   mean_age <- mean(new_age)   prev_mean_age <- get_epi(dat, at - 1, "mean_age")   age_change <- mean_age - prev_mean_age    # Update nodal attributes   dat <- set_attr(dat, "age", new_age)    # Update epidemic trackers   dat <- set_epi(dat, "mean_age", at, mean_age)   dat <- set_epi(dat, "age_change", at, age_change)    return(dat) }

code: # Add incidence rate and prevalence sim <- mutate_epi(sim, prev = i.num / num) sim <- mutate_epi(sim, ir = (si.flow / s.num) * 100)  # These new variables appear in the data.frame output as.data.frame(sim)

title: Working with Custom Attributes and Summary Statistics in EpiModel
text: epi.parameter control.net() automatically generates stratified compartment counts nodal attribute. example, track infection "risk" attribute: epi.= "risk", output includes stratified columns automatically: naming pattern {tracker}.{attribute}{value} (e.g., .num.risk0, .num.risk1, num.risk0, num.risk1).
code: set.seed(0)  nw <- network_initialize(n = 100) nw <- set_vertex_attribute(nw, "risk", rep(0:1, each = 50))  est <- netest(   nw, formation = ~edges + nodefactor("risk"),   target.stats = c(40, 20),   coef.diss = dissolution_coefs(~offset(edges), 20),  # duration = 20   verbose = FALSE )  param <- param.net(inf.prob = 0.3, act.rate = 1) init <- init.net(i.num = 5) control <- control.net(   type = "SI", nsims = 1, nsteps = 50,   epi.by = "risk",   verbose = FALSE )  sim <- netsim(est, param, init, control) d <- as.data.frame(sim) names(d) #>  [1] "sim"         "time"        "s.num"       "s.num.risk0" "s.num.risk1" #>  [6] "i.num"       "i.num.risk0" "i.num.risk1" "num"         "num.risk0"   #> [11] "num.risk1"   "si.flow"

title: Working with Custom Attributes and Summary Statistics in EpiModel
text: summary statistics beyond built-compartment counts, can define custom tracker functions pass control.net(). avoids writing full module simple calculations.

title: Working with Custom Attributes and Summary Statistics in EpiModel
text: tracker function must take exactly one argument (dat) return scalar value (numeric, logical, character). called automatically end time step, modules run. tracker computes proportion population infected: recommend pattern tracker functions: Define needed_attributes vector attribute names. Use (get_attr_list(dat, needed_attributes), { ... }) work clean environment. last expression inside becomes output. must scalar. Return output.
code: epi_s_num <- function(dat) {   needed_attributes <- c("status")   output <- with(get_attr_list(dat, needed_attributes), {     sum(status == "s", na.rm = TRUE)   })   return(output) } epi_prop_infected <- function(dat) {   needed_attributes <- c("status", "active")   output <- with(get_attr_list(dat, needed_attributes), {     pop <- active == 1     infected <- status == "i"     sum(infected & pop, na.rm = TRUE) / sum(pop, na.rm = TRUE)   })   return(output) }

title: Working with Custom Attributes and Summary Statistics in EpiModel
text: Pass tracker functions named list via .tracker.list control.net(). names become column names output data.frame: Note .tracker.list pass function objects (epi_prop_infected, epi_s_num) without parentheses—storing functions, calling . Trackers always run end simulation step. values output reflect state simulation modules completed work step.
code: some.trackers <- list(   prop_infected = epi_prop_infected,   s_num         = epi_s_num )  control <- control.net(   type = "SI",   nsims = 1,   nsteps = 50,   verbose = FALSE,   .tracker.list = some.trackers )  param <- param.net(   inf.prob = 0.3,   act.rate = 0.1 )  nw <- network_initialize(n = 50) nw <- set_vertex_attribute(nw, "race", rbinom(50, 1, 0.5)) est <- netest(   nw,   formation = ~edges,   target.stats = 25,   coef.diss = dissolution_coefs(~offset(edges), 10, 0),  # duration = 10, closed population   verbose = FALSE ) #> Starting simulated annealing (SAN) #> Iteration 1 of at most 4 #> Finished simulated annealing #> Starting maximum pseudolikelihood estimation (MPLE): #> Obtaining the responsible dyads. #> Evaluating the predictor and response matrix. #> Maximizing the pseudolikelihood. #> Finished MPLE.  init <- init.net(i.num = 10) sim <- netsim(est, param, init, control)  d <- as.data.frame(sim)  knitr::kable(tail(d, n = 15))

title: Working with Custom Attributes and Summary Statistics in EpiModel
text: debugging complex research models, may want inspect state object simulation without stopping . record_raw_object() saves R object given time step later inspection. simulation, access recorded objects netsim output: Use sparingly: Raw records truncated can consume substantial memory, especially large attribute vectors recorded many time steps. feature intended debugging, routine output collection.
code: introspect_module <- function(dat, at) {   age <- get_attr(dat, "age")    if (mean(age, na.rm = TRUE) > 50) {     obj <- data.frame(         age = age,         status = get_attr(dat, "status")     )     dat <- record_raw_object(dat, at, "old pop", obj)   }    return(dat) } sim <- netsim(est, param, init, control)  # Access raw records for simulation 1 # Returns a nested list keyed by label, then time step raw <- sim[["raw.records"]][[1]]  # Access the "old pop" data.frame recorded at step 75 raw[["old pop"]][[75]]

title: Working with Model Parameters in EpiModel
text: vignette assumes familiarity setting running network models EpiModel. See Network Modeling Epidemics (NME) course materials EpiModel Gallery background. working nodal attributes epidemic summary statistics, see companion vignette Working Custom Attributes Summary Statistics EpiModel. network objects edgelists, see Working Network Objects EpiModel. model, parameters input variables define aspects system behavior. basic SIS (Susceptible-Infected-Susceptible) model, might include infection probability, act rate, recovery rate. simple models, parameter single fixed value remains constant throughout simulation. Real-world applications, however, often require flexibility: parameters change time (e.g., represent intervention rollout behavioral change epidemic), parameters drawn distributions (sensitivity analysis calibration), large parameter sets managed via spreadsheets. vignette demonstrates implement: Scenarios: Named sets parameter changes applied start simulation specific time steps—primary mechanism time-varying parameters, counterfactual analysis, intervention modeling. Parameter input via tables: Passing many parameters data.frame, enabling spreadsheet-based parameter management. Random parameters: Distributions parameter values rather single fixed values, uncertainty quantification sensitivity analysis. Parameter control updaters (advanced): low-level mechanism underlying scenarios, exposed directly cases scenario API flexible enough.

title: Working with Model Parameters in EpiModel
text: Scenarios allow define named sets parameter values can change specific time steps simulation. recommended approach implementing time-varying parameters—example, modeling intervention reduces transmission probability partway epidemic, comparing multiple policy alternatives. research paper implementing time-varying parameter scenarios COVID-19 sexual distancing interventions published Journal Infectious Diseases. code can found .

title: Working with Model Parameters in EpiModel
text: First, set simple SIS model base case.
code: set.seed(10)  nw <- network_initialize(n = 200) est <- netest(nw,   formation = ~edges, target.stats = 60,   coef.diss = dissolution_coefs(~offset(edges), 10, 0),  # duration = 10, closed population   verbose = FALSE ) #> Starting simulated annealing (SAN) #> Iteration 1 of at most 4 #> Finished simulated annealing #> Starting maximum pseudolikelihood estimation (MPLE): #> Obtaining the responsible dyads. #> Evaluating the predictor and response matrix. #> Maximizing the pseudolikelihood. #> Finished MPLE.  param <- param.net(inf.prob = 0.9, rec.rate = 0.01, act.rate = 2) init <- init.net(i.num = 10) control <- control.net(type = "SIS", nsims = 1, nsteps = 250, verbose = FALSE)

title: Working with Model Parameters in EpiModel
text: Scenarios defined using create_scenario_list, takes specially formatted data.frame input outputs list scenarios usable EpiModel. use tibble::tribble readability, data.frame constructor works. data.frame requires two columns: .scenario.id: identifier scenario. .: time step parameter changes apply. Use 0 changes take effect simulation begins. Remaining columns parameter names. must start letter contain letters, numbers, . (underscores reserved vector indexing, described ). cell NA, EpiModel sets parameter NA—mean “leave unchanged.” example, "multiple_changes" scenario three rows sharing .scenario.id, representing single scenario parameter changes three different time points. convert data.frame usable list scenarios:
code: library(dplyr) #>  #> Attaching package: 'dplyr' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union  scenarios.df <- tribble(   ~.scenario.id, ~.at, ~inf.prob, ~rec.rate,   "base", 0, 0.9, 0.01,   "initial_change", 0, 0.2, 0.01,   "multiple_changes", 0, 0.1, 0.04,   "multiple_changes", 100, 0.9, 0.01,   "multiple_changes", 200, 0.1, 0.1 )  knitr::kable(scenarios.df) scenarios.list <- create_scenario_list(scenarios.df) str(scenarios.list, max.level = 2) #> List of 3 #>  $ base            :List of 2 #>   ..$ id                 : chr "base" #>   ..$ .param.updater.list:List of 1 #>  $ initial_change  :List of 2 #>   ..$ id                 : chr "initial_change" #>   ..$ .param.updater.list:List of 1 #>  $ multiple_changes:List of 2 #>   ..$ id                 : chr "multiple_changes" #>   ..$ .param.updater.list:List of 3

title: Working with Model Parameters in EpiModel
text: loop scenarios, using use_scenario create modified parameter object . hood, use_scenario generates parameter updaters (described Advanced section ) apply specified changes designated time steps. "multiple_changes" scenario, messages appear time steps 100 200 indicating parameters modified simulation. scenarios silent changes occur simulation begins (.= 0). Now can plot results. use base R plotting rather plot.netsim() need overlay results separate netsim runs single axis.  "initial_change" scenario shows slower epidemic base case infection probability reduced 0.9 0.2 start. "multiple_changes" scenario demonstrates three phases: Steps 1–99: Slow growth (low infection probability, higher recovery rate). Steps 100–199: Base-case parameters applied, epidemic trajectory accelerates. Steps 200–250: Infection probability drops recovery rate increases, driving rapid epidemic decline.
code: # List to hold simulation results d_list <- vector(mode = "list", length = length(scenarios.list)) names(d_list) <- names(scenarios.list)  for (scenario in scenarios.list) {   print(scenario$id)   sc.param <- use_scenario(param, scenario)   sim <- netsim(est, sc.param, init, control)   d_sim <- as.data.frame(sim)   d_sim[["scenario"]] <- scenario$id   d_list[[scenario$id]] <- d_sim } #> [1] "base" #> [1] "initial_change" #> [1] "multiple_changes" #>  #>  A MESSAGE occured in module 'epimodel.internal' at step 100 #>  #>  #> At timestep = 100 the following parameters were modified: #> 'inf.prob', 'rec.rate' #>  #>  A MESSAGE occured in module 'epimodel.internal' at step 200 #>  #>  #> At timestep = 200 the following parameters were modified: #> 'inf.prob', 'rec.rate' plot(d_list$base$time, d_list$base$i.num,      type = "l", col = 1, lwd = 2, ylim = c(0, 250),      xlab = "Time Step", ylab = "Number Infected") lines(d_list$initial_change$time, d_list$initial_change$i.num,       type = "l", col = 2, lwd = 2) lines(d_list$multiple_changes$time, d_list$multiple_changes$i.num,       type = "l", col = 3, lwd = 2) abline(v = c(100, 200), lty = 2) legend("topleft", legend = names(d_list),        col = 1:3, lwd = 2, cex = 0.9, bty = "n")

title: Working with Model Parameters in EpiModel
text: full-scale modeling projects, parameters often vectors. example, hiv.test.rate might vector length 3 containing weekly HIV screening probabilities Black, Hispanic, White persons. use vectors scenarios, create separate column element using naming convention paramname_N, N position vector: elements vector parameter must specified data.frame, even values change across scenarios (e.g., hiv.test.rate_2 constant must still included). working many parameters, recommend storing scenarios.df CSV, RDS Excel file easier sharing editing.
code: scenarios.df <- tribble(   ~.scenario.id, ~.at, ~hiv.test.rate_1, ~hiv.test.rate_2, ~hiv.test.rate_3,   "base", 0, 0.001, 0.001, 0.001,   "initial_change", 0, 0.002, 0.001, 0.002,   "multiple_changes", 0, 0.002, 0.001, 0.002,   "multiple_changes", 100, 0.004, 0.001, 0.004,   "multiple_changes", 200, 0.008, 0.001, 0.008 ) knitr::kable(scenarios.df) # CSV write.csv(scenarios.df, "scenarios.csv", row.names = FALSE) scenarios.df <- read.csv("scenarios.csv")  # RDS saveRDS(scenarios.df, "scenarios.rds") scenarios.df <- readRDS("scenarios.rds")

title: Working with Model Parameters in EpiModel
text: param.net function accepts data.frame parameters data.frame.params argument. allows passing many parameters working spreadsheet track update parameters use EpiModel. data.frame requires three columns: param: parameter name. vector parameters (length > 1), append position underscore (e.g., "p_1", "p_2"). value: parameter value (character string). type: type parameter value. three values accepted: "numeric", "logical", "character". Additional columns (e.g., details, source) may included documentation ignored EpiModel. Pass table via data.frame.params. parameters can combined named parameters maximum flexibility. case conflict, named parameters take priority data.frame:
code: df_params <- tribble(   ~param, ~value, ~type,   "hiv.test.rate_1",  "0.003",        "numeric",   "hiv.test.rate_2",  "0.102",        "numeric",   "hiv.test.rate_3",  "0.492",        "numeric",   "prep.require.lnt", "TRUE",         "logical",   "group_1",          "first",        "character",   "group_2",          "second",       "character" ) knitr::kable(df_params) param <- param.net(data.frame.params = df_params,                    other.param = c(5, 10), act.rate = 1) param #> Fixed Parameters #> --------------------------- #> hiv.test.rate = 0.003 0.102 0.492 #> prep.require.lnt = TRUE #> group = first second #> act.rate = 1 #> other.param = 5 10

title: Working with Model Parameters in EpiModel
text: Fixed parameters assume input value known certainty. practice, may want explore uncertainty parameter values propagates uncertainty epidemic outcomes. EpiModel supports drawing parameter values distributions, simulation uses different realization.

title: Working with Model Parameters in EpiModel
text: demonstrate simple SI model. define four parameters: inf.prob (remain fixed), act.rate, dummy.param, dummy.strat.param (make random ). dummy.strat.param parameter vector length 2, represent parameter stratified subpopulation.
code: nw <- network_initialize(n = 50)  est <- netest(   nw, formation = ~edges,   target.stats = 25,   coef.diss = dissolution_coefs(~offset(edges), 10, 0),  # duration = 10, closed population   verbose = FALSE ) #> Starting simulated annealing (SAN) #> Iteration 1 of at most 4 #> Finished simulated annealing #> Starting maximum pseudolikelihood estimation (MPLE): #> Obtaining the responsible dyads. #> Evaluating the predictor and response matrix. #> Maximizing the pseudolikelihood. #> Finished MPLE.  param <- param.net(   inf.prob = 0.3,   act.rate = 0.5,   dummy.param = 4,   dummy.strat.param = c(0, 1) )  init <- init.net(i.num = 10) control <- control.net(type = "SI", nsims = 1, nsteps = 5, verbose = FALSE) mod <- netsim(est, param, init, control) mod #> EpiModel Simulation #> ======================= #> Model class: netsim #>  #> Simulation Summary #> ----------------------- #> Model type: SI #> No. simulations: 1 #> No. time steps: 5 #> No. NW groups: 1 #>  #> Fixed Parameters #> --------------------------- #> inf.prob = 0.3 #> act.rate = 0.5 #> dummy.param = 4 #> dummy.strat.param = 0 1 #> groups = 1 #>  #> Model Output #> ----------------------- #> Variables: s.num i.num num si.flow #> Networks: sim1 #> Transmissions: sim1 #>  #> Formation Statistics #> -----------------------  #>       Target Sim Mean Pct Diff Sim SE Z Score SD(Sim Means) SD(Statistic) #> edges     25     16.8    -32.8  1.114  -7.364            NA          2.49 #>  #>  #> Duration Statistics #> -----------------------  #>       Target Sim Mean Pct Diff Sim SE Z Score SD(Sim Means) SD(Statistic) #> edges     10    8.697  -13.032   0.27  -4.819            NA         0.605 #>  #> Dissolution Statistics #> -----------------------  #>       Target Sim Mean Pct Diff Sim SE Z Score SD(Sim Means) SD(Statistic) #> edges    0.1    0.065  -34.615  0.022  -1.608            NA         0.048

title: Working with Model Parameters in EpiModel
text: draw parameters distributions, use random.params argument param.net. two approaches.

title: Working with Model Parameters in EpiModel
text: Define generator function random parameter: .randoms list contains three elements: act.rate uses param_random function factory provided EpiModel (see ?param_random). simulation samples one three values equal probability. dummy.param zero-argument function returns single draw Beta(1, 2) distribution. dummy.strat.param zero-argument function returns vector length 2, element drawn normal distribution different mean standard deviation. element must named parameter fills must function taking arguments, returning vector correct length parameter. print parameter list running model, random parameters appear function definitions since values yet realized. running 3 simulations, inf.prob remains “Fixed Parameters” random parameters 3 realized values (one per simulation). vector parameter dummy.strat.param shows <list> realization vector length 2. inspect realized values: can merged epidemic output analysis parameter-outcome relationships:
code: my.randoms <- list(   act.rate = param_random(c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75)),   dummy.param = function() rbeta(1, 1, 2),   dummy.strat.param = function() {     c(rnorm(1, 0.05, 0.01),       rnorm(1, 0.15, 0.03))   } )  param <- param.net(   inf.prob = 0.3,   random.params = my.randoms )  param #> Fixed Parameters #> --------------------------- #> inf.prob = 0.3 #>  #> Random Parameters #> (Not drawn title: Working with Model Parameters in EpiModel
text: Generator functions draw parameter independently. parameters need correlated—example, using Latin hypercube sampling one parameter derived another—use pre-defined parameter sets instead. Define data.frame row complete set correlated parameter values: row contains parameter values used together single simulation. Vector parameters use paramname_N suffix convention scenarios. means underscores reserved appear parameter names . Save parameter set .randoms list reserved name param.random.set: inf.prob parameter remains fixed, act.rate remains independently random, two remaining parameters drawn correlated sets data.frame. Verify correlated sets sampled together:
code: n <- 5  related.param <- data.frame(   dummy.param = rbeta(n, 1, 2) )  related.param$dummy.strat.param_1 <- related.param$dummy.param + rnorm(n) related.param$dummy.strat.param_2 <- related.param$dummy.param * 2 + rnorm(n)  related.param #>   dummy.param dummy.strat.param_1 dummy.strat.param_2 #> 1  0.40042189           1.6831189           1.0748786 #> 2  0.73021770           2.3839536           1.3420617 #> 3  0.05306671           0.6405278          -0.7281755 #> 4  0.90490846          -1.3179645          -0.4500947 #> 5  0.48049243           0.7020599           2.3109663 my.randoms <- list(   act.rate = param_random(c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75)),   param.random.set = related.param )  param <- param.net(   inf.prob = 0.3,   random.params = my.randoms )  param #> Fixed Parameters #> --------------------------- #> inf.prob = 0.3 #>  #> Random Parameters #> (Not drawn yet) #> --------------------------- #> act.rate = <function> #> param.random.set = <data.frame> ( dimensions: 5 3 ) control <- control.net(type = "SI", nsims = 3, nsteps = 5, verbose = FALSE) mod <- netsim(est, param, init, control)  mod #> EpiModel Simulation #> ======================= #> Model class: netsim #>  #> Simulation Summary #> ----------------------- #> Model type: SI #> No. simulations: 3 #> No. time steps: 5 #> No. NW groups: 1 #>  #> Fixed Parameters #> --------------------------- #> inf.prob = 0.3 #> groups = 1 #>  #> Random Parameters #> --------------------------- #> dummy.param = 0.9049085 0.4804924 0.4004219 #> dummy.strat.param = <list> #> act.rate = 0.75 0.75 0.75 #>  #> Model Output #> ----------------------- #> Variables: s.num i.num num si.flow #> Networks: sim1 ... sim3 #> Transmissions: sim1 ... sim3 #>  #> Formation Statistics #> -----------------------  #>       Target Sim Mean Pct Diff Sim SE Z Score SD(Sim Means) SD(Statistic) #> edges     25   25.933    3.733  0.502   1.859         0.757         1.944 #>  #>  #> Duration Statistics #> title: Working with Model Parameters in EpiModel
text: scenarios system described uses internal updater module implement parameter changes designated time steps. section describes underlying updater mechanism directly. Use scenario API flexible enough—example, need relative (function-based) parameter changes time-varying control settings.

title: Working with Model Parameters in EpiModel
text: updater list two required elements: (time step change) param (named list new parameter values): updater sets inf.prob 0.3 act.rate 0.5 time step 10. Multiple updaters combined list:
code: list(   at = 10,   param = list(     inf.prob = 0.3,     act.rate = 0.5   ) ) #> $at #> [1] 10 #>  #> $param #> $param$inf.prob #> [1] 0.3 #>  #> $param$act.rate #> [1] 0.5 list.of.updaters <- list(   list(     at = 100,     param = list(       inf.prob = 0.3,       act.rate = 0.3     )   ),   list(     at = 125,     param = list(       inf.prob = 0.01     )   ) )

title: Working with Model Parameters in EpiModel
text: Pass updater list param.net via .param.updater.list argument: plot shows inflection points two parameter change points. step 100, inf.prob act.rate increase 0.1 0.3, accelerating transmission. step 125, inf.prob drops 0.01, slowing epidemic.
code: param <- param.net(  inf.prob = 0.1,  act.rate = 0.1,  .param.updater.list = list.of.updaters ) init <- init.net(i.num = 10) control <- control.net(  type = "SI",  nsims = 1,  nsteps = 200,  verbose = FALSE ) nw <- network_initialize(n = 100) est <- netest(   nw,   formation = ~edges,   target.stats = 50,   coef.diss = dissolution_coefs(~offset(edges), 10, 0),  # duration = 10, closed population   verbose = FALSE ) #> Starting simulated annealing (SAN) #> Iteration 1 of at most 4 #> Finished simulated annealing #> Starting maximum pseudolikelihood estimation (MPLE): #> Obtaining the responsible dyads. #> Evaluating the predictor and response matrix. #> Maximizing the pseudolikelihood. #> Finished MPLE. mod <- netsim(est, param, init, control) #>  #>  A MESSAGE occured in module 'epimodel.internal' at step 100 #>  #>  #> At timestep = 100 the following parameters were modified: #> 'inf.prob', 'act.rate' #>  #>  A MESSAGE occured in module 'epimodel.internal' at step 125 #>  #>  #> At timestep = 125 the following parameters were modified: #> 'inf.prob' plot(mod, mean.smooth = FALSE) abline(v = c(100, 125), lty = 2, col = "grey50")

title: Working with Model Parameters in EpiModel
text: updater can include optional verbose element. TRUE (default), EpiModel prints message describing changes:
code: list(   at = 10,   param = list(     inf.prob = 0.3,     act.rate = 0.5   ),   verbose = TRUE ) #> $at #> [1] 10 #>  #> $param #> $param$inf.prob #> [1] 0.3 #>  #> $param$act.rate #> [1] 0.5 #>  #>  #> $verbose #> [1] TRUE

previous_headings: Time-Varying Parameters and Control Settings (Advanced) > Incorporating Updaters
title: Working with Model Parameters in EpiModel
text: Instead setting parameter fixed new value, can define function transforms current value. useful multiplicative changes logit-scale adjustments. updater, act.rate set fixed value 0.5 . inf.prob, instead value, provide function. time step 10, EpiModel applies function current value inf.prob. inf.prob set 0.1 param.net, function computes plogis(qlogis(0.1) + log(2)) = 0.1818, doubles odds transmission keeping result probability scale.
code: list(   at = 10,   param = list(     inf.prob = function(x) plogis(qlogis(x) + log(2)),     act.rate = 0.5   ) ) #> $at #> [1] 10 #>  #> $param #> $param$inf.prob #> function (x)  #> plogis(qlogis(x) + log(2)) #>  #> $param$act.rate #> [1] 0.5

title: Working with Model Parameters in EpiModel
text: Control settings can also changed simulation using updater mechanism. control updater uses control element (instead param): example turns network resimulation step 100 disables model verbosity step 125. Pass control updaters control.net via .control.updater.list, paralleling parameter updaters passed param.net via .param.updater.list:
code: list.of.updaters <- list(   list(     at = 100,     control = list(       resimulate.network = FALSE     )   ),   list(     at = 125,     control = list(       verbose = FALSE     )   ) ) control <- control.net(  type = "SI",  nsims = 1,  nsteps = 200,  verbose = TRUE,  .control.updater.list = list.of.updaters )

title: Working with Network Objects in EpiModel
text: vignette covers work network objects, edgelists, partnership histories EpiModel network models custom extension modules. assumes familiarity setting running network models netsim() extension API. See Network Modeling Epidemics (NME) course materials EpiModel Gallery background. working nodal attributes epidemic summary statistics, see companion vignette Working Custom Attributes Summary Statistics EpiModel.

title: Working with Network Objects in EpiModel
text: EpiModel supports two storage modes networks, controlled tergmLite parameter control.net(): Full mode (tergmLite = FALSE, default): Networks stored networkDynamic objects, preserve complete history edge activations deactivations. allows extraction full dynamic network simulation. However, networkDynamic objects consume substantial memory. tergmLite mode (tergmLite = TRUE): Networks stored lightweight networkLite objects containing current edgelist nodal attributes. provides 20–50x performance improvement much lower memory usage, making essential large-scale research models. trade-get_network() returns networkLite (snapshot) rather full dynamic network history. extension modules work identically modes access networks EpiModel’s accessor functions rather manipulating network objects directly.

title: Working with Network Objects in EpiModel
text: Inside custom module, use get_network() set_network() accessors work network objects: accessors handle tergmLite vs. full-mode distinction internally, module code works storage modes. full mode, get_network() returns networkDynamic object; tergmLite mode, returns networkLite. practice, rarely need access network objects directly. Instead, use edgelist accessor functions described , also work correctly storage modes.
code: # Get the network for layer 1 nw <- get_network(dat, network = 1)  # After modifying a network, set it back dat <- set_network(dat, network = 1, nw = nw)

title: Working with Network Objects in EpiModel
text: netsim call, extract network objects get_network(): full mode, get_network() returns networkDynamic object. tergmLite mode, returns networkLite object representing final state. collapse arguments available full mode. Note: Network objects saved output save.network TRUE control.net() (default full mode). tergmLite mode, network history save.
code: sim <- netsim(est, param, init, control)  # Extract the network from simulation 1, network layer 1 nw <- get_network(sim, sim = 1, network = 1)  # Collapse to a static cross-section at time step 50 (full mode only) nw_at_50 <- get_network(sim, sim = 1, collapse = TRUE, at = 50)

title: Working with Network Objects in EpiModel
text: transmission matrix records every transmission event simulation: returns data.frame columns including (time step), sus (ID newly infected node), inf (ID infecting node), infDur (duration infector’s infection), transProb, actRate, finalProb. Transmission matrices saved default (save.transmat = TRUE control.net()).

title: Working with Network Objects in EpiModel
text: Current edgelists set active partnerships present time step. commonly used network data structures inside extension modules.

title: Working with Network Objects in EpiModel
text: get_edgelist() returns current edgelist given network two-column matrix positional IDs: row active partnership. Column 1 positional ID “head” node; column 2 “tail” node. function works identically storage modes.

title: Working with Network Objects in EpiModel
text: get_edgelists_df() combines edgelists multiple network layers single data.frame network column: returned data.frame columns head, tail, network.
code: # All networks el_all <- get_edgelists_df(dat, networks = NULL)  # Specific networks el_12 <- get_edgelists_df(dat, networks = c(1, 2))

title: Working with Network Objects in EpiModel
text: discordant edgelist identifies partnerships partners different values status attribute—key data structure modeling transmission. example, SI model, discordant edges one partner susceptible infected: returned data.frame columns head, tail, head_status, tail_status, network. orderings captured: node (infected) partnered node B (susceptible), edge appears regardless “head” vs “tail” underlying network. See also discord_edgelist() original, simpler version function used built-models.
code: disc_el <- get_discordant_edgelist(   dat,   status.attr = "status",   head.status = "i",   tail.status = "s" )

title: Working with Network Objects in EpiModel
text: EpiModel uses two ways reference nodes: position: Think like row number spreadsheet. dat$attr$active[3] accesses third node’s value directly. standard way look node information fast. model 100 nodes, positions range 1 100. nodes depart, may dropped vectors, freeing position new arrivals. unique_id: globally unique integer attribute assigned node creation never reused. Slower look , allows referencing nodes already departed. Used cumulative edgelists attribute histories. Conversion two systems handled internally EpiModel. get_unique_ids() get_posit_ids() functions perform conversion. See help("unique_id-tools", package = "EpiModel") details.

title: Working with Network Objects in EpiModel
text: cumulative edgelist historical record edges network, including time steps edge started stopped. allows querying current past partnerships—essential contact tracing, partnership duration analysis, reachability analysis.

title: Working with Network Objects in EpiModel
text: Cumulative edgelist tracking must explicitly enabled control.net(): Without cumulative.edgelist = TRUE, calls update_cumulative_edgelist() silently nothing calls get_cumulative_edgelist() return empty tibble.
code: control <- control.net(   type = "SI",   nsims = 1,   nsteps = 100,   cumulative.edgelist = TRUE,       # Enable tracking during simulation   save.cumulative.edgelist = TRUE,  # Save in output after simulation   verbose = FALSE )

title: Working with Network Objects in EpiModel
text: cumulative edgelist must updated time step network resimulation. custom module end initialization: truncate argument controls memory usage: truncate = Inf: Keep full history edges (removal). truncate = 0 (default): Keep currently active edges. Use need track active edge start times. truncate = N: Remove edges ended N time steps ago. balances historical depth memory use. update networks multi-layer model:
code: dat <- update_cumulative_edgelist(dat, network = 1, truncate = Inf) for (n_network in seq_len(dat$num.nw)) {   dat <- update_cumulative_edgelist(dat, n_network, truncate = 100) }

previous_headings: Cumulative Edgelist > Accessing the Cumulative Edgelist
title: Working with Network Objects in EpiModel
text: returned tibble four columns: head: unique_id first node. tail: unique_id second node. start: time step edge formed. stop: last time step edge active, NA edge still active. edge start = 5 stop = 12 existed steps 5 12, inclusive. get_cumulative_edgelist() always returns tibble four columns, even edges exist cumulative edgelist enabled—cases, tibble zero rows.

previous_headings: Cumulative Edgelist > Accessing the Cumulative Edgelist
title: Working with Network Objects in EpiModel
text: networks argument accepts vector network indices NULL (networks). returned data.frame adds network column identifying network layer edge belongs .

title: Working with Network Objects in EpiModel
text: get_partners() extracts partners specified nodes cumulative edgelist: Arguments: dat: main list object. index_posit_ids: vector positional IDs nodes interest (“indexes”). networks: network layers search (NULL ). truncate: include edges ended within many steps (filters edge age). .active.nodes: TRUE, exclude partnerships inactive (departed) nodes. output similar get_cumulative_edgelists_df() columns index partner (containing unique IDs) instead head tail. Note indexes specified positional ID output uses unique IDs, since partners may include nodes already departed.
code: partner_list <- get_partners(     dat,     index_posit_ids,     networks = NULL,     truncate = Inf,     only.active.nodes = FALSE )

title: Working with Network Objects in EpiModel
text: get_cumulative_degree() counts number distinct partners node tracked history: returns data.frame columns index_pid (positional ID) degree (cumulative partner count). wraps get_partners() counts unique partners per index.
code: cum_degree <- get_cumulative_degree(     dat,     index_posit_ids = 1:50,     networks = NULL,     truncate = Inf,     only.active.nodes = FALSE )

title: Working with Network Objects in EpiModel
text: Reachability functions determine nodes can connected chains partnerships time window. useful outbreak investigation (forward reachability: node infected?) source tracing (backward reachability: infected node?). functions operate cumulative edgelist objects directly—dat—typically used post-simulation analysis.

title: Working with Network Objects in EpiModel
text: Returns list two elements: reached: named list element contains set nodes reachable index node chains partnerships active [from_step, to_step]. lengths: matrix one row per node one column per time step (plus initial column), showing reachable set grows time. allows back-calculating distance reachable node. Nodes identified unique ID output (named node_ID). Nodes edges analysis period excluded output; forward reachable set just (size 1).
code: el_cuml <- get_cumulative_edgelist(dat, network = 1)  fwd <- get_forward_reachable(   el_cuml,   from_step = 1,   to_step = 52,   nodes = c(10, 25, 42),  # NULL for all nodes with edges   dense_optim = "auto" )

what: Mathematical Modeling of Infectious Disease Dynamics
title: Mathematical Modeling of Infectious Disease Dynamics
text: EpiModel R package simulating mathematical models infectious disease dynamics. Epidemic model classes include deterministic compartmental models, stochastic individual-contact models, stochastic network models. Network models use robust statistical methods exponential-family random graph models (ERGMs) Statnet suite R packages. Standard templates epidemic modeling include SI, SIR, SIS disease types. EpiModel features API extending templates address novel research aims. EpiModel part broader platform infectious disease modeling. Visit epimodel.org full ecosystem, learning pathway, team, project information.

what: Model Classes and Infectious Disease Types
title: Mathematical Modeling of Infectious Disease Dynamics — EpiModel-package
text: EpiModel provides functionality three classes epidemic models: Deterministic Compartmental Models: continuous-time models solved using ordinary differential equations. EpiModel allows easy specification sensitivity analyses compare multiple scenarios model across different parameter values. Stochastic Individual Contact Models: novel class individual-based, microsimulation models developed add random variation components transmission system, infection recovery vital dynamics (arrivals departures). Stochastic Network Models: underlying statistical framework temporal exponential random graph models (ERGMs) recently developed Statnet suite software R, network models epidemics simulate edge (e.g., partnership) formation dissolution stochastically according specified statistical model, disease spread across network. EpiModel supports three infectious disease types run across three classes. Susceptible-Infectious (SI): two-state disease life-long infection without recovery. HIV/AIDS one example, although case common model infection stages separate compartments. Susceptible-Infectious-Recovered (SIR): three-stage disease one life-long recovery immunity infection. Measles one example, modern models disease also require consideration vaccination patterns population. Susceptible-Infectious-Susceptible (SIS): two-stage disease one may transition back forth susceptible infected states throughout life. Examples include bacterial sexually transmitted diseases like gonorrhea. basic disease types may extended arbitrarily complex way simulate specific diseases research questions.

title: Mathematical Modeling of Infectious Disease Dynamics — EpiModel-package
text: EpiModel uses three model setup functions model class input necessary parameters, initial conditions, control settings: param.dcm(), param.icm(), param.net() used input epidemic parameters three model classes. Parameters include rate contacts acts actors, probability transmission per contact, recovery demographic rates models include transitions. init.dcm(), init.icm(), init.net() used input initial conditions class. main conditions limited numbers , applicable, specific agents population infected recovered simulation outset. control.dcm(), control.icm(), control.net() used specify remaining control settings simulation. core controls base model types include disease type, number time steps, number simulations. Controls also used input new model functions (DCMs) new model modules (ICMs network models) allow user simulate fully original epidemic models EpiModel. See documentation specific control functions help pages. models parameterized, functions simulating epidemic models : dcm() deterministic compartmental models. icm() individual contact models. Network models simulated three-step process: netest() estimates statistical model network structure (.e., partnerships form dissolve time given parameterization processes). function wrapper around ergm tergm functions ergm tergm packages. current statistical framework model simulation called "egocentric inference": target statistics summarizing formation dissolution processes collected egocentric sample population. netdx() runs diagnostics dynamic model fit simulating base network time ensure model fits targets formation dissolution. netsim() simulates stochastic network epidemic models, given network model fit netest(). function requires model fit object along parameters, initial conditions, control settings defined .

EpiModel: R Package Mathematical Modeling Infectious Disease Networks. Journal Statistical Software. 2018; 84(8): 1-47. doi:10.18637/jss.v084.i08 . also developed two extension packages modeling specific disease dynamics. title: Definition for fuzzynodematch ERGM Term — InitErgmTerm.fuzzynodematch
text: ERGM user term written allow generalized homophily.attr term argument specify character vertex attribute encoding "venues" associated node.  split argument specify string separates different "venues" attribute value node, handled strsplit fixed = TRUE. example, split "|" (default), attribute value given node "a12|b476", associated venues node "a12" "b476".  empty string "" interpreted "venues". binary term argument FALSE (default), change statistic -toggle number unique venues associated nodes (informally speaking, described number venues two nodes "match"); binary TRUE, change statistic -toggle 1 venue associated nodes, 0 otherwise.

title: Fast Version of network::add.vertices for Edgelist-formatted Network — add_vertices
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library("EpiModel") nw <- network_initialize(100) formation <- ~edges target.stats <- 50 coef.diss <- dissolution_coefs(dissolution = ~offset(edges), duration = 20) x <- netest(nw, formation, target.stats, coef.diss, verbose = FALSE)  param <- param.net(inf.prob = 0.3) init <- init.net(i.num = 10) control <- control.net(type = "SI", nsteps = 100, nsims = 5,                        tergmLite = TRUE)  # networkLite representation after initialization dat <- crosscheck.net(x, param, init, control) dat <- initialize.net(x, param, init, control)  # Check current network size attributes(dat$el[[1]])$n  # Add 10 vertices dat$el[[1]] <- add_vertices(dat$el[[1]], 10)  # Check new network size attributes(dat$el[[1]])$n } # }

title: Convert transmat Infection Tree into a phylo Object — as.phylo.transmat
text: x object class transmat, output get_transmat(). vertex.exit.times Optional numeric vector providing time departure vertices, used scale lengths branches reaching tips. Index position vector corresponds network id. NA indicates departure, branch extend end tree. ... arguments (unused).

title: Convert transmat Infection Tree into a phylo Object — as.phylo.transmat
text: phylo class object.

title: Convert transmat Infection Tree into a phylo Object — as.phylo.transmat
text: Converts transmat() object containing information history simulated infection ape::phylo object representation suitable plotting tree ape::plot.phylo(). infection event becomes 'node' (horizontal branch) resulting phylo tree, network vertex becomes 'tip' tree. infection events labeled vertex ID infector make possible trace path infection. infection timing information included position phylo-nodes, lines tips drawn max time value +1 (unless vertex.exit.times passed effectively assumes vertices active end simulation). transmat contains multiple infection seeds (multiple trees separate root nodes), function return list class multiPhylo, element phylo object. See ape::read.tree().

title: Convert transmat Infection Tree into a phylo Object — as.phylo.transmat
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(13)  # Fit a random mixing TERGM with mean degree of 1 and mean edge # duration of 20 time steps nw <- network_initialize(n = 100) formation <- ~edges target.stats <- 50 coef.diss <- dissolution_coefs(dissolution = ~offset(edges), duration = 20) est <- netest(nw, formation, target.stats, coef.diss, verbose = FALSE) #> Starting simulated annealing (SAN) #> Iteration 1 of at most 4 #> Finished simulated annealing #> Starting maximum pseudolikelihood estimation (MPLE): #> Obtaining the responsible dyads. #> Evaluating the predictor and response matrix. #> Maximizing the pseudolikelihood. #> Finished MPLE.  # Parameterize the epidemic model as SI with one infected seed param <- param.net(inf.prob = 0.5) init <- init.net(i.num = 1) control <- control.net(type = "SI", nsteps = 40, nsims = 1, verbose = FALSE)  # Simulate the model mod1 <- netsim(est, param, init, control)  # Extract the transmission matrix tm <- get_transmat(mod1) head(tm, 15) #> # A tibble: 15 × 8 #> # Groups:   at, sus [15] #>       at   sus   inf network infDur transProb actRate finalProb #>    <int> <int> <int>   <int>  <dbl>     <dbl>   <dbl>     <dbl> #>  1     2     7    31       1      3       0.5       1       0.5 #>  2     2    94    31       1      3       0.5       1       0.5 #>  3     4    20    31       1      5       0.5       1       0.5 #>  4     4    33    94       1      2       0.5       1       0.5 #>  5     5    34    94       1      3       0.5       1       0.5 #>  6     5    72    33       1      1       0.5       1       0.5 #>  7     6     6    34       1      1       0.5       1       0.5 #>  8     6    36    72       1      1       0.5       1       0.5 #>  9     6    95    33       1      2       0.5       1       0.5 #> 10     7    40    34       1      2       0.5       1       0.5 #> 11     8     1    95       1      2       0.5       1       0.5 #> 12     8    48     7       1      6       0.5       1       0.5 #> 13     8    60    40       1      1       0.5       1       0.5 #> 14     8    68    95       1      2       0.5       1       0.5 #> 15     8    89     6       1      2       0.5       1       0.5  # Convert to phylo object and plot tmPhylo <- as.phylo.transmat(tm) par(mar = c(1,1,1,1)) plot(tmPhylo, show.node.label = TRUE,               root.edge = TRUE,               cex = 0.75)  # }

title: RColorBrewer Color Ramp for EpiModel Plots — brewer_ramp
text: RColorBrewer provides easy access helpful color palettes, built-palettes limited set colors existing palette. function expands palette size number colors filling gaps. Also, colors within "div" "seq" set palettes whose colors light (close white) deleted default better visualization plots.

title: RColorBrewer Color Ramp for EpiModel Plots — brewer_ramp
code: # Shows a 100-color ramp for 4 RColorBrewer palettes par(mfrow = c(2, 2), mar=c(1, 1, 2, 1)) pals <- c("Spectral", "Greys", "Blues", "Set1") for (i in seq_along(pals)) {  plot(1:100, 1:100, type = "n", axes = FALSE, main = pals[i])  abline(v = 1:100, lwd = 6, col = brewer_ramp(100, pals[i])) }

title: Plot Compartment Diagram for Epidemic Models — comp_plot
text: comp_plot function provides visual summary epidemic model specific time step. information contained comp_plot summary functions model, presented graphically compartment flow diagram. dcm class plots, specify model run number model contains multiple runs, sensitivity analysis. icm netsim class plots, run argument used; plots show means standard deviations across simulations specified time step. plots currently limited one-group models three model classes. functionality may expanded future software releases.

title: Plot Compartment Diagram for Epidemic Models — comp_plot
code: ## Example 1: DCM SIR model with varying act.rate param <- param.dcm(inf.prob = 0.2, act.rate = 5:7,                    rec.rate = 1/3, a.rate = 1/90, ds.rate = 1/100,                    di.rate = 1/35, dr.rate = 1/100) init <- init.dcm(s.num = 1000, i.num = 1, r.num = 0) control <- control.dcm(type = "SIR", nsteps = 25, verbose = FALSE) mod1 <- dcm(param, init, control) comp_plot(mod1, at = 25, run = 3)   ## Example 2: ICM SIR model with 3 simulations param <- param.icm(inf.prob = 0.2, act.rate = 3, rec.rate = 1/50,                    a.rate = 1/100, ds.rate = 1/100,                    di.rate = 1/90, dr.rate = 1/100) init <- init.icm(s.num = 500, i.num = 1, r.num = 0) control <- control.icm(type = "SIR", nsteps = 25,                        nsims = 3, verbose = FALSE) mod2 <- icm(param, init, control) comp_plot(mod2, at = 25, digits = 1)

Model solutions fractional time steps may obtained setting number 0 1. odemethod Ordinary differential equation (ODE) integration method, default "lsoda" (see deSolve::ode options). dede TRUE, use delayed differential equation solver, allows time-lagged variables. new.mod running base model type, function new model simulated (see details). sens.param TRUE, evaluate arguments parameters initial conditions length greater 1 sensitivity analyses, one model run per value. FALSE, one model run parameters initial conditions arbitrary length (model may error unless model function designed accommodate vectors). print.mod TRUE, print model form console. verbose TRUE, print model progress console. ... additional control settings passed model.

title: Control Settings for Deterministic Compartmental Models — control.dcm
text: EpiModel 2.5.5, default ODE solver changed "rk4" (fixed-step Runge-Kutta 4) "lsoda" (adaptive step-size). "lsoda" method automatically adjusts internal step size, providing numerical stability across wide range parameter values. fixed-step "rk4" solver produce numerical instability (negative compartment sizes, NaN values) models high transmission rates stiff dynamics. Users require previous behavior can set odemethod = "rk4" explicitly.

text: type Disease type modeled, choice "SI" Susceptible-Infected diseases, "SIR" Susceptible-Infected-Recovered diseases, "SIS" Susceptible-Infected-Susceptible diseases. nsteps Number time steps simulate model . must positive integer equal final step simulation. simulation restarted start argument, number must least one greater argument's value. start models network resimulation, time point start simulation. restarted simulations, must one greater final time step prior simulation must less value nsteps. nsims total number disease simulations. ncores Number processor cores run multiple simulations , using future framework multisession. See future.use.plan future backends. resimulate.network TRUE, resimulate network time step. required epidemic demographic processes impact network structure (e.g., vital dynamics). parameter controls whether resim_nets.FUN performs actual network resimulation; affect order modules executed (see module.order). Setting tergmLite = TRUE forces resimulate.network = TRUE warning. tergmLite Logical indicating usage either tergm (tergmLite = FALSE), tergmLite (tergmLite = TRUE). Default FALSE. TRUE, resimulate.network automatically set TRUE (warning user explicitly set FALSE). cumulative.edgelist TRUE, calculates cumulative edgelist within network simulation module. used tergmLite used entire networkDynamic object used. truncate.el.cuml Number time steps cumulative edgelist retain. See help update_cumulative_edgelist options. attr.rules list containing  rules setting attributes incoming nodes, one list element per attribute set (see details ). epi.character vector length 1 containing nodal attribute subgroup stratified prevalence summary statistics calculated. nodal attribute must contained network model formation formula, otherwise ignored. initialize.FUN Module initialize model time 1, default function initialize.net. resim_nets.FUN Module resimulate network time step, default function resim_nets. summary_nets.FUN Module extract summary statistics network time step, default function summary_nets. infection.FUN Module simulate disease infection, default function infection.net. recovery.FUN Module simulate disease recovery, default function recovery.net. departures.FUN Module simulate departure exit, default function departures.net. arrivals.FUN Module simulate arrivals entries, default function arrivals.net. nwupdate.FUN Module handle updating network structure nodal attributes due exogenous epidemic model processes, default function nwupdate.net. prevalence.FUN Module calculate disease prevalence time step, default function prevalence.net. verbose.FUN Module print simulation progress screen, default function verbose.net. module.order character vector module names lists modules order evaluated within time step. NULL, modules evaluated follows: first new modules supplied ... order listed, built-modules order listed arguments . initialize.FUN always run first verbose.FUN always run last. Module ordering independent resimulate.network: specified order always respected regardless whether network resimulation enabled. default ordering, resim_nets.FUN runs infection.FUN, network resimulated transmission evaluated time step. save.nwstats TRUE, save network statistics data frame. statistics saved specified nwstats.formula argument. nwstats.formula right-hand sided ERGM formula includes network statistics interest, default formation formula terms. Supports multilayer specification. save.transmat TRUE, complete transmission matrix saved simulation end. save.network TRUE, networkDynamic networkLite object saved simulation end. save.run TRUE, run sublist dat saved, allowing simulation restart output. save.cumulative.edgelist TRUE, cumulative.edgelist saved simulation end. save.character vector elements netsim_dat main data list save simulation. One example base models attribute list, "attr", final time step. verbose TRUE, print model progress console. verbose.int Time step interval printing progress console, 0 prints completion status entire simulation positive integer x prints progress every x time steps. default print progress time step. skip.check TRUE, skips default error checking structure consistency parameter values, initial conditions, control settings running base epidemic models. Setting FALSE recommended running models new modules specified. raw.output TRUE, netsim output list raw data (one per simulation) instead cleaned formatted netsim object. future.use.plan FALSE, netsim use multisession used workers = ncores parallelization. TRUE, netsimwill use user defined plan fromglobalEnv. Finally, can take output future::tweak()call setup user defined temporary plan withinnetsim`. can useful distributed computation (HPC). tergmLite.track.duration TRUE, track duration information models tergmLite simulations. Supports multilayer specification. set.control.ergm Control arguments passed ergm::simulate_formula.network. netsim, used initializing network edapprox = TRUE. simulations netsim use tergm. Supports multilayer specification. set.control.tergm Control arguments passed tergm::simulate_formula.network. See help file netdx details examples specifying parameter. Supports multilayer specification. save.diss.stats TRUE, netsim compute save duration dissolution statistics plotting printing, provided save.network TRUE, tergmLite FALSE, dissolution model homogeneous. dat.updates Either NULL, single function taking arguments dat, , network, list functions length one greater number networks simulated, function list taking arguments dat . dat main netsim_dat class object, current timestep, network index indicating current position within sequence network (re)simulations time step. single function passed, called first network simulated network simulated, network = 0L first network simulated network = ith network simulated. list functions passed, first function called first network simulated, + 1th function called ith network simulated.
